description: FutSpring Frontend Redesign & Polish — green gradient design system, real landing page, polished NavBar with dark mode and user avatar dropdown, per-page UX improvements, ErrorBoundary, 404 page, scroll-to-top, route transitions, shadcn/ui, and full responsive/accessibility audit.

userStories.title: Install shadcn/ui and add missing UI primitives
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shadcn/ui fully installed so I can use Dialog, DropdownMenu, Sheet, Avatar, Badge, Skeleton, Popover, and Separator throughout the redesign.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `npx shadcn@latest init` in frontend/ — select existing Tailwind config, output to src/components/ui
Add these components via shadcn CLI or manual creation: avatar, badge, skeleton, dialog, dropdown-menu, sheet, separator
Existing ui/button.tsx, ui/card.tsx, ui/input.tsx, ui/label.tsx, ui/tabs.tsx remain intact (do not overwrite without verifying compatibility)
tailwind.config.js darkMode remains ["class"]
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Green gradient button variant
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable green gradient style so all primary CTAs have a consistent visual identity.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a `gradient` variant to ui/button.tsx (or button-variants.ts): classes `bg-gradient-to-br from-green-500 to-emerald-600 text-white hover:from-green-400 hover:to-emerald-500 dark:from-green-400 dark:to-emerald-500 transition-all duration-150`
Apply the `gradient` variant to primary CTA buttons across all existing pages: 'Confirm Attendance', 'Create Pelada', 'Sort Teams', 'Start Session'
Secondary and destructive buttons are unchanged
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: NavBar — logo, layout, and user avatar dropdown
userStories.description: As a user, I want a polished NavBar with branding and quick access to my profile and logout so navigation feels professional.
acceptanceCriteria: NavBar is sticky top with `backdrop-blur-sm bg-background/80 border-b`
Left: FutSpring wordmark with Trophy lucide icon, links to /home
Center (desktop, hidden on mobile): nav link 'Home' → /home with active green underline using useLocation
Right: dark mode toggle button (Sun/Moon icon, aria-label='Toggle dark mode') + user avatar button
User avatar button opens a shadcn DropdownMenu containing: non-clickable header with username + email, 'View Profile' → /profile/:id, 'Logout'
Avatar shown as shadcn Avatar component — uploaded image or initials fallback
Dropdown closes on outside click and Escape
Existing logout logic preserved inside dropdown
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: NavBar — dark mode toggle with localStorage persistence
userStories.description: As a user, I want a sun/moon toggle in the NavBar so I can switch between light and dark mode and have my preference remembered.
acceptanceCriteria: On app mount (main.tsx or App.tsx): read localStorage.getItem('theme'), apply 'dark' class to document.documentElement if value is 'dark'
NavBar toggle button: on click, flip the 'dark' class on document.documentElement and write 'dark' or 'light' to localStorage
Icon: Sun when in dark mode (click to go light), Moon when in light mode (click to go dark)
Button has aria-label='Toggle dark mode'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: NavBar — mobile hamburger menu with Sheet drawer
userStories.description: As a mobile user, I want a hamburger button that opens a slide-in drawer with all nav items so the NavBar doesn't overflow on small screens.
acceptanceCriteria: Menu lucide icon button shown only on mobile (block sm:hidden)
Desktop avatar + nav links hidden on mobile (hidden sm:flex)
Clicking Menu opens a shadcn Sheet (side='left' or side='right') containing: 'Home' nav link, dark mode toggle, 'View Profile' link, 'Logout' button
Sheet closes on any nav item click
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Global — ErrorBoundary, 404 page, ScrollToTop, and route transitions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need global infrastructure components so the app handles crashes gracefully, unknown routes show a 404, scroll resets on navigation, and pages fade in.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx as a React class component with componentDidCatch; renders 'Something went wrong' + 'Reload' button (window.location.reload())
Each lazy-loaded route in App.tsx is wrapped in its own <ErrorBoundary> (not a single top-level wrapper)
Create src/pages/NotFoundPage.tsx with a football-themed 404 message and a back-to-home button; add a path='*' catch-all route in App.tsx
Create src/components/ScrollToTop.tsx using useEffect + useLocation to call window.scrollTo(0,0) on route change; render it once inside <Router> in App.tsx
Add a CSS animation in index.css: @keyframes fadeIn { from { opacity: 0 } to { opacity: 1 } }; each page root div gets class 'animate-[fadeIn_150ms_ease-in]' or a shared `page-enter` class
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Landing page — auth redirect + hero section
userStories.description: As a guest visitor, I want a compelling hero section; as an authenticated user I want to be redirected to /home immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: The / route checks auth state on mount; if authenticated, redirect to /home before landing page renders (no flash)
Full-width min-h-screen hero section with bg-gradient-to-br from-green-600 to-emerald-800
Headline: 'Your football crew, organized.' — text-4xl md:text-6xl font-bold text-white
Subheadline: short description in text-lg text-green-100
Two CTA buttons: 'Get Started' → /auth?tab=register (white filled), 'Sign In' → /auth (outlined white)
Decorative element on right side desktop (football emoji or SVG), hidden on mobile
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Landing page — features section, how-it-works section, and footer
userStories.description: As a guest visitor, I want to understand FutSpring's key features and how it works before signing up, and see a complete footer.
acceptanceCriteria: Features section: 3–4 cards in responsive grid (1 col mobile, 2 tablet, 4 desktop); light green tint background (bg-green-50 dark:bg-green-950/20); each card has lucide icon + title + short description; features: 'Schedule Sessions', 'Auto-balance Teams', 'Track Stats', 'Group Chat'
How It Works section: 3 steps with numbered green circles — '1. Create a Pelada', '2. Invite Friends', '3. Play & Track Stats'
Footer: dark background (bg-gray-900 text-gray-400), 'FutSpring © 2025', link to /auth; not sticky
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: HomePage — pelada cards grid with skeleton and empty state
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my peladas as visual cards in a responsive grid with skeletons during load and a helpful empty state.
acceptanceCriteria: Page header: 'My Peladas' (text-2xl font-bold tracking-tight) + '+ New Pelada' green gradient button top-right
Responsive grid: 1 col mobile, 2 col md:, 3 col lg:

Each card (rounded-xl border shadow-sm): banner image or green gradient ⚽ placeholder, pelada name (font-bold text-lg), day of week + time, member count Badge, next session date (nearest SCHEDULED/CONFIRMED daily) or 'No upcoming session'
Clicking card navigates to /pelada/:id
While loading: show 3 skeleton cards (Skeleton component, matching card shape)
Empty state (0 peladas): football icon + 'You're not in any pelada yet. Create one to get started.' + green gradient CTA button
'+ New Pelada' button opens existing CreatePeladaModal
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: PeladaDetailPage — banner header overlay and tabs layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want the pelada name overlaid on the banner image and the page content organised in tabs so it feels polished and navigable.
acceptanceCriteria: Banner: add bg-gradient-to-t from-black/70 to-transparent overlay absolutely over banner image; pelada name in white text-2xl font-bold over overlay
Edit (Pencil) and Delete (Trash2) lucide icon buttons top-right of banner, visible only to admin/creator (isAdmin); Edit triggers existing EditPeladaModal
Content area uses existing shadcn Tabs component (ui/tabs.tsx) with three tabs: Members, Sessions, Ranking — default active: Members
Each tab renders its existing content section
On desktop: chat sidebar stays on the right; on mobile: chat sidebar hidden, replaced by a fixed bottom-right floating button (MessageCircle icon) that toggles the chat panel
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: PeladaDetailPage — member 3-dot dropdown and clickable member names
userStories.description: As an admin, I want a 3-dot dropdown per member row to manage members cleanly, and member names should link to their profiles.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace inline 'Make Admin' / 'Remove' buttons with a MoreVertical lucide icon button per row
Icon button opens a shadcn DropdownMenu with: 'Make Admin' / 'Remove Admin' (toggled by current role), 'Remove from Pelada'
DropdownMenu calls existing API functions — no backend changes
DropdownMenu closes on outside click and Escape
3-dot button shown only when current user isAdmin
Member names rendered as <Link to={`/profile/${member.id}`}>
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: PeladaDetailPage — sessions status borders, empty state, and ranking medals
userStories.description: As a user, I want session rows to have status-colored left borders and the ranking tab to show medal icons for top 3.
acceptanceCriteria: Each session row has left border color: SCHEDULED=yellow, CONFIRMED=green, IN_COURSE=blue, FINISHED=gray, CANCELLED=red (use border-l-4 with Tailwind color classes)
Sessions tab empty state: football icon + 'No sessions scheduled yet.'
Ranking tab row 1: 🥇, row 2: 🥈, row 3: 🥉 in the rank column; rows 4+ show numeric rank
Existing sorting logic unchanged
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: DailyDetailPage — header polish with live badge and admin action bar
userStories.description: As a user, I want the daily header to clearly show the date, status, and whether the session is live; as an admin, I want a visually distinct action bar.
acceptanceCriteria: Date as large title: text-2xl font-bold tracking-tight
shadcn Badge next to title showing current status text
Time shown as subtitle below: text-sm text-muted-foreground
If status is IN_COURSE: pulsing green dot (animate-pulse bg-green-500 rounded-full w-2 h-2 inline-block) + 'Live' text next to the badge
Admin action bar wrapper: rounded-xl border p-3 flex flex-wrap gap-2 bg-muted/40; entire bar gated behind daily.isAdmin
'Confirm Daily' button: yellow gradient (bg-gradient-to-br from-yellow-400 to-amber-500 text-white)
'Start Session' button: green gradient variant
'Cancel Daily' button: destructive outlined
'Enter Results / Finalize' button: green gradient variant
Buttons wrap correctly on mobile (flex-wrap)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: DailyDetailPage — confirmed players section and teams section polish
userStories.description: As a user, I want confirmed players to show avatars and links to profiles, and team cards to have colored headers so they are visually distinct.
acceptanceCriteria: Confirmed players: each row shows shadcn Avatar (image or initials) + player name as <Link to={`/profile/${player.id}`}> + position Badge + star rating
Section header shows '(N confirmed)' count
Teams section: each team card has a colored header bar — Team A: bg-green-100 dark:bg-green-900/30, Team B: bg-blue-100 dark:bg-blue-900/30, additional teams cycle other colors
Players inside team card: shadcn Avatar + name + stars
Move/Swap button styled as small icon button with ArrowLeftRight lucide icon
Team cards stack to single column on mobile
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: DailyDetailPage — finished state hero, trophy cards, and IN_COURSE CTA
userStories.description: As a user reviewing a finished session, I want champion photo and awards displayed prominently; as an admin during a live session, I want a prominent CTA to enter results.
acceptanceCriteria: Finished state: champion photo moved to top as full-width hero image (if it exists)
Awards section styled as trophy cards with gold (#FFD700) / silver (#C0C0C0) accent borders
Medal icons 🥇🥈🥉 on top 3 rows in league table and player stats
Player stats section collapsed by default with existing toggle
IN_COURSE state with no results entered: show a prominent card 'Session is live — enter results when ready' with Enter Results button (green gradient); card has pulsing green border
IN_COURSE card shown only to isAdmin; non-admins see a simpler 'Session is live' message without the button
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: ProfilePage — peladas grid section and stats card green accent
userStories.description: As a user viewing a profile, I want to see the peladas that person belongs to and have the stats cards aligned with the green design system.
acceptanceCriteria: New 'Peladas' section below existing profile content
Fetch from /api/v1/peladas, filter to peladas where the profile user is a member (check members list for userId match)
Show pelada cards in a grid (2 col mobile, 3 col desktop): banner/placeholder + pelada name; clicking navigates to /pelada/:id
Empty state: 'Not in any peladas yet.'
Skeleton loading while fetching
Each stat card has subtle green top border: border-t-2 border-green-500
Edit modal verified to work in dark mode (no invisible text, correct bg colors)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Global — toast audit, skeleton loading, and accessibility
userStories.description: As a user, I want every async action to have success/error toasts, skeletons instead of spinners, and accessible interactive elements.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit all API mutation calls (create, update, delete, confirm, start, cancel, finalize) across HomePage, PeladaDetailPage, DailyDetailPage, ProfilePage, AuthPage — every mutation has both toast.success(...) and toast.error(...)
GET fetch errors also have toast.error(...)

Replace any remaining spinner/bare 'Loading...' text with shadcn Skeleton components matching content shape in: PeladaDetailPage (banner + member list), DailyDetailPage (player list), ProfilePage (avatar + stats) — HomePage skeletons already done in US-009
Buttons that trigger async ops show disabled + opacity-50 during the request
All icon-only buttons have aria-label
All interactive elements have focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus rings
All buttons meet minimum h-10 touch target
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend — Repository Query Methods for User Timeline & Match History
userStories.description: As a developer, I need new query methods on UserDailyStatsRepository to fetch user stats by date range and full history, so the new service methods have efficient data access.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `findByUserOrderByDailyDailyDateDesc(User user)` to UserDailyStatsRepository
Add `@Query` JPQL method `findByUserAndDateRange(@Param('user') User user, @Param('from') LocalDate from, @Param('to') LocalDate to)` returning List<UserDailyStats> sorted ASC by dailyDate
`mvn compile` passes with no errors
No existing repository methods are altered

userStories.title: Backend — GET /api/v1/users/{userId}/stats/timeline Endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need a timeline endpoint so the chart can display time-series stats filtered by a date range.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `UserStatsTimelineDTO` with inner class `TimelinePoint` (fields: date LocalDate, goals int, assists int, wins int, matchesPlayed int)
Add `getTimeline(Long userId, LocalDate from, LocalDate to, String callerEmail)` to StatsService using the new repository method from US-018
Add `@GetMapping('/{id}/stats/timeline')` to UserController accepting `@RequestParam @DateTimeFormat(iso=DATE) LocalDate from, to` and `Authentication authentication`
Returns 200 with `{ points: [...] }` sorted ascending by date
Returns 404 if userId not found
Returns empty points array (not 404) if user has no stats in range
`mvn compile` passes; existing `/stats` endpoint unaffected

userStories.title: Backend — GET /api/v1/users/{userId}/stats/matches Endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need a match history endpoint so the statistics table can show a per-daily session breakdown.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `UserMatchHistoryDTO` with inner class `MatchRow` (fields: dailyId Long, date LocalDate, peladaId Long, peladaName String, goals int, assists int, matchesPlayed int, wins int, result String)
`result` computed on backend: wins > 0 → 'W', matchesPlayed > 0 && wins == 0 → 'L', else '—'
Add `getMatchHistory(Long userId, String callerEmail)` to StatsService annotated `@Transactional(readOnly = true)`
Add `@GetMapping('/{id}/stats/matches')` to UserController with `Authentication authentication`
Returns rows sorted by date DESC
Returns empty rows array if user has no sessions
`mvn compile` passes

userStories.title: Frontend — Install shadcn Table and Chart Components
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the shadcn Table and Chart components installed so they can be used in the redesigned profile page.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `npx shadcn@latest add table` in frontend/ → `src/components/ui/table.tsx` exists and exports Table, TableHeader, TableBody, TableHead, TableRow, TableCell
Run `npx shadcn@latest add chart` in frontend/ → `src/components/ui/chart.tsx` exists and exports ChartContainer, ChartTooltip, ChartTooltipContent
`bun run build` or `tsc --noEmit` passes with no type errors
No existing components are overwritten or broken

userStories.title: Frontend — New API Functions and TypeScript Types for Profile Stats
userStories.description: As a developer, I need typed API functions and TypeScript interfaces for the two new backend endpoints so the frontend can consume them safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Add to `src/types/stats.ts` (or create if missing): interfaces TimelinePoint, UserStatsTimelineDTO, MatchHistoryRow, UserMatchHistoryDTO
Add to `src/api/users.ts`: `getUserStatsTimeline(userId: number, from: string, to: string): Promise<UserStatsTimelineDTO>`
Add to `src/api/users.ts`: `getUserMatchHistory(userId: number): Promise<UserMatchHistoryDTO>`
`tsc --noEmit` passes

userStories.title: Frontend — ProfilePage: Remove Purple Gradient and Full-Width Layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want the profile page to use the full screen width with a clean flat design so it feels modern and spacious.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove `container max-w-2xl mx-auto` wrapper from main content area
Replace purple gradient inline style fallback on banner div with `bg-muted` class
All content sections use `px-6 lg:px-12` horizontal padding
ProfileSkeleton updated to match new full-width layout (remove container wrapper from skeleton too)
No purple gradient visible at any viewport width
`tsc --noEmit` passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — ProfilePage: KPI Dashboard Section with shadcn Card
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see 4 KPI cards at the top of my profile so I can quickly understand my overall performance at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace old StatCard component with shadcn Card-based KpiCard using CardHeader and CardContent
4 cards: Goals, Assists, Matches Played, Win Rate
Win Rate computed as `Math.round((wins / matchesPlayed) * 100)` — shows 0% if matchesPlayed is 0
Green top border accent on each card: `border-t-2 border-t-green-500`
Grid: `grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-4`
Old StatCard component and old wins count card removed
`tsc --noEmit` passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — ProfilePage: Configurable Chart Section with shadcn Chart
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my stats visualized over time with a configurable area chart so I can track performance trends.
acceptanceCriteria: Area chart using shadcn ChartContainer + recharts AreaChart (replaces old BarChart)
Metric selector using shadcn Select: Goals / Assists / Wins — one series shown at a time
Time range selector using shadcn Select: Last 7 days / Last 1 month / Last 3 months
Chart fetches from `getUserStatsTimeline` on mount and on range change
Shadcn Skeleton shown while loading; empty state message shown when timeline is empty
Old BarChart and puskasDates (Puskas Award History) section removed from ProfilePage
`tsc --noEmit` passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — ProfilePage: Match History Table with shadcn Table
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a detailed breakdown of all my past sessions so I can review my individual performance over time.
acceptanceCriteria: shadcn Table with 6 columns: Date, Pelada, Goals, Assists, Matches, Result
Pelada column renders as `<Link to={'/pelada/:id'}>` with hover underline
Result column shows shadcn Badge: default variant for W, secondary for L, outline for —
5 skeleton rows (each cell has Skeleton) shown while loading
Empty state: 'No match history yet.' text shown if rows array is empty
Section header 'Match History' above the table
`tsc --noEmit` passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Frontend — ProfilePage: Full-Width Polish and Consistent Section Layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want all four sections to feel visually consistent and polished so the redesigned profile page looks professional end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: All 4 sections (KPI cards, Chart, Peladas, Match History) wrapped in `<section className='px-6 lg:px-12 mb-10'>`
Consistent section headings: `<h2 className='text-xl font-semibold mb-4'>` with `<Separator className='mb-6' />` below each
Peladas cards get `hover:shadow-md transition-shadow`
ProfileSkeleton updated to include skeleton blocks for all 4 sections
`page-enter` animation class preserved on outermost wrapper
`tsc --noEmit` passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
